expectations.text: Output shows all 3 stages: advocate evaluation, critic challenges, and arbiter decision
expectations.evidence: All 3 stages: Stage 1 Advocate Report, Stage 2 Critic Report, Stage 3 Arbiter Decision

expectations.text: Advocate evaluates strengths for both PostgreSQL and MongoDB
expectations.evidence: Advocate covers Option A PostgreSQL (A-001 to A-006) and Option B MongoDB (A-007 to A-011)

expectations.text: Analysis discusses the variable attribute challenge (JSONB vs document model)
expectations.evidence: A-002 covers PostgreSQL JSONB with GIN indexes. A-007 covers MongoDB native document model. C-001 challenges JSONB as a compromise. Arbiter Dispute 1 resolves this.

expectations.text: Analysis weighs the team's PostgreSQL expertise vs MongoDB learning curve
expectations.evidence: A-001 highlights team expertise with PostgreSQL. C-004 rates zero MongoDB experience as critical risk. Arbiter Dispute 3 calls this the decisive factor.

expectations.text: Analysis considers faceted search requirements for both options
expectations.evidence: A-004 covers PostgreSQL faceted search (GIN, pg_trgm, materialized views). A-010 covers MongoDB $facet. C-002 challenges PostgreSQL faceted search engineering effort.

expectations.text: Arbiter provides a clear recommendation with confidence level
expectations.evidence: Recommendation: PostgreSQL, Confidence: High. Includes schema example and indexing strategy.

expectations.text: Includes conditions under which the recommendation would change
expectations.evidence: 4 conditions listed: team had MongoDB experience, catalog 5M+ SKUs, schemas changed daily, team grew to 10+ engineers
